_comment: Integrity/validation checks per data domain, as required by the feasibility brief. 'implemented' checks already exist in the codebase (cited); 'recommended' checks do not exist yet and are new, small additions — not blockers to any current hypothesis.

future_timestamps.recommended: explicit check that no bar timestamp exceeds the fetch-time wall clock — currently relies on provider correctness, not independently verified

holiday_gaps.recommended: FX/metals/indices weekend and holiday gaps are currently tolerated implicitly (no bar = no signal that day) rather than explicitly validated as expected-vs-anomalous; crypto trades 24/7 so any gap there IS an anomaly worth flagging

maintenance_gaps.recommended: exchange maintenance windows (crypto) or broker feed outages (cTrader) are not distinguished from genuine data gaps today

outliers.reference: core/data_confidence.py cross-checks independent providers on carrier symbols and flags disagreement

impossible_values.reference: core/data_validator.validate_ohlcv() — malformed OHLC relationship check (e.g. low > high)

negative_oi.implemented: not applicable to this domain — see crypto_positioning domain

funding_spikes.implemented: not applicable to this domain — see crypto_positioning domain

corporate_actions.implemented: not applicable — FX/metals/crypto/index-CFDs have no corporate actions

contract_rollovers.recommended: index CFDs (US30/NAS100/SPX500) may be subject to broker-side contract roll conventions this project has not audited for silent price-series discontinuities at roll dates

exchange_migrations.reference: H023 IS a documented real instance of exactly this class of bug — cTrader's migration silently changed FX from zero-volume (Twelve Data era) to nonzero tick-volume, changing Wyckoff's live behavior undetected until code inspection caught it (2026-07-18)

missing_timestamps.reference: daily series, gaps are typically legitimate (weekends/holidays), not validated as anomalous vs. expected

holiday_gaps.note: CBOE/FRED both follow US market holidays; not anomalies

maintenance_gaps.implemented: not applicable — daily batch series, no live maintenance window concept

outliers.recommended: no explicit sanity bound on daily VIX/yield moves — a genuine data error (e.g. a FRED series revision glitch) would not currently be caught before entering the engine

impossible_values.recommended: e.g. VIX < 0 or yields implausibly outside historical range are not explicitly rejected

contract_rollovers.implemented: not applicable — index-level series, not futures contracts

exchange_migrations.reference: 2026-07-17 GLD Yahoo ETF proxy -> FRED GOLDAMGBD228NLBM LBMA gold fixing migration, done deliberately for correctness

missing_timestamps.recommended: no explicit check for a skipped weekly release (CFTC has delayed/skipped COT during past government shutdowns — a real, expected gap that should be distinguished from a scraper failure)

duplicate_timestamps.note: current 12-week rolling cache structure implicitly avoids duplicates by construction; a new deep-archive backfill script would need its own explicit dedup on (symbol, report_date)

holiday_gaps.implemented: not applicable — weekly cadence, not daily

outliers.recommended: large week-over-week net-positioning swings are not flagged; could indicate a parser misalignment against CFTC's fixed-width column layout rather than a real market event

impossible_values.recommended: net position values inconsistent with total open interest for the same contract are not cross-checked

negative_oi.implemented: not applicable — COT reports net speculator positioning, not raw OI, though a sanity cross-check against total OI would be a reasonable addition

contract_rollovers.implemented: not applicable — COT aggregates across all contract months for a given commodity

exchange_migrations.recommended: CFTC occasionally renames/reclassifies instruments across years — a deep-archive backfill script must handle this explicitly or silently mis-map older records

missing_timestamps.reference: scripts/probe_crypto_positioning_data.py confirmed no gaps across 6,574 funding-rate records and 3,092 Fear&Greed records

duplicate_timestamps.reference: _paginate_forward() tracks actual timestamp progress specifically to avoid the duplicate/stall failure mode found in Bybit's 200-cap-regardless-of-limit behavior

future_timestamps.reference: causal_context_at() strictly filters to settlement/publish timestamps before the decision bar — enforced, tested (tests/test_crypto_positioning_modulator.py)

holiday_gaps.implemented: not applicable — crypto trades 24/7, funding settles on a fixed 8h schedule with no holiday exceptions

maintenance_gaps.recommended: exchange maintenance windows on Binance could in principle produce a missed 8h funding settlement; not explicitly distinguished from a genuine data gap

outliers.recommended: extreme funding-rate spikes (e.g. during a real squeeze event) are exactly the signal the modulator is designed to react to, not noise to filter — but an impossible value (e.g. funding rate outside Binance's own +/-0.75% cap) is not explicitly validated

impossible_values.recommended: no explicit range check against Binance's documented funding-rate cap

negative_oi.implemented: not applicable — OI was dropped from H019 entirely

funding_spikes.note: compute_funding_zscore() is specifically built to react to these, not suppress them

contract_rollovers.implemented: not applicable — perpetual futures have no expiry/rollover

exchange_migrations.implemented: not applicable — single exchange (Binance) used throughout, no migration occurred

missing_timestamps.implemented: not applicable — event-driven (article publication), no fixed schedule to have gaps against

duplicate_timestamps.recommended: same story re-syndicated across sources can produce near-duplicate (symbol, published_at) rows — dedup logic should be verified/added before H021's actual evaluation run

outliers.recommended: no bound check on sentiment_score staying within MarketAux's documented -1..1 range

exchange_migrations.implemented: not applicable — single source (MarketAux) in use

missing_timestamps.note: bar_start_ms() floors every trade to its 15-min bar; a bar with zero trades is simply never emitted (DeltaAccumulator.is_empty()), not a gap to detect after the fact

duplicate_timestamps.implemented: not applicable — append-only stream, one bar per completed interval per symbol by construction

future_timestamps.recommended: trade_ts_ms is trusted as-sent by Binance; no explicit sanity check against local wall-clock time

maintenance_gaps.reference: reconnect-with-backoff on any WebSocket drop (collect_symbol()); a genuine multi-minute Binance-side outage would show as a gap in the JSONL, not currently flagged/alerted separately from a normal quiet period

outliers.recommended: no sanity bound on trade price/qty vs. a recent rolling range — a bad tick from the exchange would currently flow straight into the bar aggregate

impossible_values.reference: parse_agg_trade() rejects malformed JSON/missing fields/non-numeric price via a try/except returning None; does not reject a syntactically valid but economically implausible value (e.g. price=0)

contract_rollovers.implemented: not applicable — spot aggTrade stream, not a futures contract

exchange_migrations.implemented: not applicable — single exchange, single stream format

missing_timestamps.implemented: not applicable — one row per real fill, no fixed schedule

duplicate_timestamps.reference: one row per real broker fill by construction; dry-run fills explicitly excluded (storage/execution_quality.py:30-32)

future_timestamps.implemented: not applicable — timestamps are set at write time from the live system clock

holiday_gaps.implemented: not applicable — gaps simply mean no trade occurred, not a data-quality issue

outliers.recommended: no explicit bound on slippage_r magnitude — an extreme value could be a real adverse fill or a computation bug (e.g. a stale entry_price); worth a sanity range check before H105's Stage 1 analysis runs

impossible_values.recommended: e.g. a fill price on the wrong side of the stated direction's intended price is not explicitly rejected before being averaged into the slippage distribution
